entry.title: Function EDI_MM_INVOICE_PROCESSING was not executed successfully - Field Item-PurchaseOrderItemID
entry.content:
Hello,

After setting Intercompany Sales Order Processing - International - 1MX, I have an error in Monitoring and error handling app:

  • Function EDI_MM_INVOICE_PROCESSING was not executed successfully
  • Field Item-PurchaseOrderItemID is required
  • Processing error
  • Error in inbound processing

Could you please someone explain why and what should be corrected? In 1MX we are not working with purchase orders.

Thank you,

Simona

@s4hanacloudsap

entry.title: Not possible to activate the tenant in desktop agent
entry.content:
Hello,

We are having troubles to activate the tenant in Desktop Agent for last two days. Even if we restart the agent and try to active the tenant again, nothing happens and the status remains as "Connecting".

We were changing some information in communication arrangement earlier. Is it possible it is related to that change..? 

Thank you in advance.

Martina

Good Afternoon,

There are 47 tables with this Table Field:
I will perform the select and come back 🙂

Have a nice day.

entry.title: Re: A new generation of CDS views: CDS view entities
entry.content:
Hi Andrea,

thanks for the detailed information, I learned a lot!

I have another question about authorization: If our CDS Views no longer have SQLView, how is authorization controlled?

For example, if a customer created a query using View Entity in S/4HAHA (private cloud) and then opens it in AfO (Analysis for Office), How to ensure that only people with the correct authorization can open the correct query in AfO?

Thanks a lot and looking forward to any feedback! 

 

Best regards,

Rundong

entry.title: SE78 image not printed on Adobe form in batch job
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm facing the following problem with printing of Adobe form into Spool in a batch job.

Standard PY program allows to enter the name of SE78 image on selection screen which then is put on the form. It works perfectly in SAP GUI when the form is shown.

However when the same program is ran as batch job and output saved into spool the form with all the data is there but no image is embedded.

Is this a known issue with SE78 and Adobes in batch ?

Since it's a standard program and form I can't change it.

I can see only that the form has an XML interface which is filled by the program with binary data in the field where image resides. They use cl_ssf_xsf_utilities=>get_bds_graphic_as_bmp  to get the image and then SSFC_BASE64_ENCODE  to convert it.

Can this be somehow made to work in batch?

 

Regards

Lukasz

entry.title: Re: Sap B1 Hana SP 2402 Registering B1WebServer into SLD...
entry.content:
Hi selimkocak,

In that case, you can create an incident for SAP Business One Support Team to get it investigated.

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content: Hi from Ukraine!

entry.title: Theme displayed incorrectly on Launchpad; correctly in Theme Designer and preview
entry.content:
We have a custom theme for Fiori Launchpad on a S4 2022 system (SAPUI5 1.108.24). After upgrading another (testing) system to S4 2023 (SAPUI5 1.120.0) this theme is displayed incorrectly on Fiori Launchpad, but correctly in the Theme Designer and the Theme Designer preview.

The theme has been removed and re imported both in the Theme Designer and in the Theme Tool, but this does not help. Invalidating Cache in the Theme Tool (UI5/THEME_TOOL) does not help. How can we fix this?

entry.title: SAP IQ NLS-The Cold Storage Solution for SAP BW/4HANA
entry.content:
Introduction –

 As we know B systems  contain a large volume of data that is only accessed rarely. The data is either never or rarely needed in Data Warehouse processes or for analysis. The main challenge of implementing a multi-temperature memory strategy is to seamlessly integrate the warm and cold memory areas and to make these areas invisible to the outside, in order to ensure that all required functions are applied to this data. SAP offers various solutions, which help to reduce TCO  SAP IQ NLS is one of them to reduce load by cold data

 

SAP IQ based Nearline storage can be used to offload the data and reduce main-memory data footprint further. Typically, the data is sliced based on the time dimensions or it can be completely moved into the cold storage DB. The read access to IQ NLS is in most cases much faster than READ access to the traditional databases. The unique advantage of such a native solution is that the performance of the BW query that requires the data from the Sybase IQ store can be optimized using HANA SDA (Smart Data Access). Smart data access enables remote data access as if they are local tables in HANA without copying data into HANA. It is  highly optimized, high-performance and decision-support server designed specifically for mission-critical business intelligence, analytics, data warehousing and Component Integration Services within SAP .Sybase IQ provide direct access to relational and non-relational databases on mainframe, UNIX, or Windows server. SAP IQ (formerly known as SAP Sybase IQ or Sybase IQ; IQ for Intelligent Query) is a column-based, petabyte scale, relational database software system used for business intelligence, data warehousing, and data marts

Its primary function is to analyze large amounts of data in a low-cost, highly available environment. SAP IQ is often credited with pioneering the commercialization of column-store technology. At the foundation of SAP IQ lies a column store technology that allows for speed compression and ad-hoc analysis. SAP IQ has an open interface approach towards its ecosystem. SAP IQ is also integrated with SAP's Business Intelligence portfolio of products to form an end-to-end business analytics software stack, and is an integral component of SAP's In-Memory Data Fabric Architecture and Data Management Platform.

 Architecture :

Below diagram is sap IQ data base Architecture -

Communication between BW and SAP IQ  via NLS interface

Pre-installation Tasks:

  • Check for Operating System Patches- check snote - 3060790
  • Increase the Swap Space- Recommended minimum swap space is 1 GB.
  • Check Port Availability for SAP IQ Cockpit -  HTTP Port: 4282 HTTPS Port: 4283 ,RMI Port: 4992 ,TDS Port: 499
  • Verify Network Functionality- verify that the server where you plan to install SAP IQ can use TCP/IP to communicate with other devices on your network.
  • Disable HugePages - Remove the HugePages options from /etc/ sysctl.conf and restore the kernel to the default configuration
  • Transparent HugePages - Disable Transparent HugePages to avoid SAP IQ performance problems.
  • Set the Kernel Parameters-Set the number of available semaphore identifiers to at least 4096.
  • Create the SAP IQ Account
  • Download Installation Files  login on sap market place and download require software based on you infrastructure

Installation   -

The below flowchart gives you an overview of the major tasks to install and implement a SAP IQ system on an Azure Linux VM. Some configuration and post steps need to be done by BW team.

Install SySAM

  Given below are step to install SySAM

Unselect  un-relevant  component and enter option 11 SySAM License server

Choose  1 Typical

 

 

 Now you can see below directory.

 Install SAP IQ Server

Start IQ

Command : start_iq -n utility -x "tcpip{port=34238}" -su *****

Installing client on BW dB and App server

Install SDA

Check all environment variable as per IQ installation  document

Configuration

Logon to BW system and update RSADMIN table using report SAP_RSADMIN_MAINTAIN

Create DBCO Connection

Transaction DBACOCKPIT

Add

And  test connectivity

References –

SAP IQ Installation and Update Guide for Linux (SAP IQ 16.1 SP 05 Document Version: 1.0.0 – 2023-12-15)

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/managing-large-sap-bw-on-hana-systems-sap-hana-enterprise-cloud-hec/ba-p/13363789

SAP IQ - Wikipedia

entry.title: Re: Blog it Forward – Pierre Col
entry.content:
@PierreCol  All the @erickgomez-tech´s Interviews are not technical, he tries to establish a friendly chat, the topic is to know the person, the professional that works on sap, for example he ask about your favorite pastime, Let me prupose that you tow guys can stablish a little inteview and then you can share by linkedin, by this blog, etc. and Erick can send you some cuestions and when you have a little time you answer them and return them to Erick. This could be the switch that could starts your first step to learn spanish, and for Erick to learn English. This is only my suggest, and my point of view.

I think that it could be interesting.

Best Regards.

entry.title: Debug DTP Filter Routine
entry.content:
Dear SCN Community ,

I have been going through the SCN Blogs for Debugging DTP Filter routines where I found to put a BREAK-POINT in code which is not possible in a Production Environment where a complex routine needs to be simulated . For this case I have followed an approach which will be helpful :

Here we will discuss a simple approach to debug a DTP filter routine which doesn’t need a hard coded break point.

  1. Go to the DTP and in Execute Tab select Processing Mode as Serially in the Dialog process (for Debugging) – Expert Mode (To make sure no data gets loaded once debugging is completed).
  2. Now go to class CL_RSBC_FILTER_CMD and method PROCESS_SEL_ROUTINE. We will see a standard piece of code with LOOP AT l_t_varseltab . Put a debug point at the line .
  3. DTP filter routines are a type of Form with naming convention C_<FIELD_NAME>.In screenshot 2. put a debug point on the PERFORM statement. PERFORM Key word is used to call the form.
  4. Now in DTP screen, put /H in the Transaction screen and press enter. Now click on simulate.
  5. If there are multiple filter routines in the DTP, debugger will call routines 1 by 1. Once the variable is picked up by debugger for which we need to debug the code, go inside the perform statement. We will be landed on the DTP filter code which we wanted to debug.

Assign points if its useful for you ! 🙂

Best Regards,

Suraj Grewal

entry.title: How to attach documents to a Journal Entry within SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud from ABAP.
entry.content:
Introduction

In this blog, we describe a method to attach document files to a Journal Entry (BKPF) instance within the same SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud using ABAP.

 
 
This requires us to expose and consume the Attachment API as described in the SAP Business Accelerator HUB - Attachments.
 
I belive this blog can be very useful since existing documentation primarily focuses on exposing the services, but we encounter conceptual problems when consuming them locally.
 
This method is also valid for a S/4HANA Private Cloud but has been implemented and tested only in S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Steps

  1. Create the Outbound Service. In Eclipse, and within your development package, follow these steps to create a new Outbound Service:

    • Add name and description.
    • Select the type HTTP.
    • Set the Default Path Prefix for the Attachment method AttachmentContentSet, as follows: "/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV/AttachmentContentSet".

  2. Create the Communication Scenario. Still in Eclipse and under your development package, create a new Communication Scenario as follows:

    • Add name and description.
    • In the Outbound tab, add the previously created service.

    • Use the top-right 'Publish Locally' button to publish the communication scenario.

  3. Create the Communication User. Navigate to the Maintain Communication User app and create a new user. Remember to copy the username and password, as we will need them to configure the communication systems.



  4. Assign the new communication user to our local Communication System. In the Communication System app, select our local system using the top button labeled "Own SAP Cloud System."



    • Copy the hostname, as it will be needed in the next step.
    • In edit mode, assign the previously created user as the User for Inbound Communication and save.
  5. Create a Communication System to consume the service. Go to the Communication Systems App, create a new system, fill the ID and description.



    • Complete hostname field with the value obtained in the previous step (local hostname).

    • Assign the previously created user as Outbound User, and save.

  6. Create the Communication Arrangement to expose the service. Navigate to the Communication Arrangement App, create a new one, or check if one exists, based on the Scenario SAP_COM_0002: Finance - Posting Integration. This scenario has sufficient permissions to attach document files to a Journal Entry instance.

     

    • After selecting or creating the communication arrangement, ensure that the communication system is assigned to your local system. Also, add the created communication user as Inbound Communication User.

       



    • We don't need the Outbound Services of the Arrangement for this requirement, so unless necessary for other reasons, deactivate these services by unchecking the Active flag.

  7. Create the Communication Arrangement to consume the service. Create a new Communication Arrangement based on our scenario created in step 2, assign the communication system created and assign the outbound communication user.

     


    After saving, you can verify if the user/system relationship has been correctly configured using the Check Connection button.

    If you get an error, please check the Outbound User has the right password in Communication System.

  8. Consume the Attachment API from an ABAP Class.

  • You can consume the API using ABAP with the following code.

 

    TRY.
        DATA(lo_destination) = cl_http_destination_provider=>create_by_comm_arrangement(
                                 comm_scenario  = 'ZCS_ATTACHMENT' "Communication Arrangement
                                 service_id     = 'ZOS_API_ATTACHMENT_REST' "Outbound Service).

        DATA(lo_http_client) = cl_web_http_client_manager=>create_by_http_destination( i_destination = lo_destination ).

        "Set CSRF Token
        lo_http_client->set_csrf_token(  ).

        DATA(lo_request) = lo_http_client->get_http_request( ).

        lo_request->set_header_field( i_name = 'Slug' i_value = iv_filename )."Filename (with extension)
        lo_request->set_header_field( i_name = 'BusinessObjectTypeName' i_value = iv_bo_type ). "BKPF
        lo_request->set_header_field( i_name = 'LinkedSAPObjectKey' i_value = iv_bo_key ). "Journal Entry concatenate key fields.
        lo_request->set_content_type( content_type = iv_mimetype ). "Mimetype
        lo_request->set_binary( i_data = iv_attachment ). "File content in binary

        DATA(lo_response) = lo_http_client->execute( i_method = if_web_http_client=>post ).


        DATA(ls_status) = lo_response->get_status( ).

        IF ls_status-code NE 201.
          "Catch error (the attachment hasn't be created)
        ENDIF.

      CATCH cx_web_message_error INTO DATA(lx_web).
         "Catch error (the service is misconfigured)
    ENDTRY.

 

Please take a look to "iv_" variables and replace them with valid information. or include the code into a class method with these variables, all of them are strings.

entry.title: abapGit SSSLERR_PEER_CERT_UNTRUSTED
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

sorry most probably for a stupid question, but it seems that github has recently change the SSL certificates and since than I could not add ABAP Git Repository in the ADT due to this error:

HTTP error 421 occured: SSL handshake with github.com:443 failed: SSSLERR_PEER_CERT_UNTRUSTED (-102)
Peer's X.509 certificate (chain) validation failed

Any experience with this issue? Is ADT using my user or computer certificates stores to check the trusted CAs? Or is it some config in Eclipse?

Many thanks for any hints.

Milan

entry.title: Hello SAP world!
entry.content: Nice to meet you!

entry.title: Navigating Business Transformation: Capgemini's Expertise in Data and AI Analytics with SAP
entry.content:
At Capgemini, we understand the evolving needs of businesses and the importance of using technology and innovation to stay ahead. Through our comprehensive approach encompassing Strategy and Transformation, Applications and Technology, Engineering, and Operations, we deliver tailored solutions to drive growth and efficiency.

 

Our SAP unit is strategically positioned at the forefront of data and AI/analytics integration, empowering our clients in their journey towards digital transformation. By leveraging SAP's robust cloud portfolio and integrated solution stack, we equip businesses, particularly the office of the CFO, with the tools needed to navigate the complexities of modern finance and beyond.

 

In a world where data and analytics are paramount, CFOs are increasingly tasked with overseeing the broader spectrum of business operations. Beyond traditional finance KPIs, they must focus on areas such as customer satisfaction, forward planning, and advanced data analytics. Recognizing this shift, Capgemini harnesses its deep industry knowledge to develop Line of Business (LoB) focused content centered around SAP Datasphere.

 

Our collaborative efforts extend beyond SAP, integrating cutting-edge partner technologies like Databricks and Google Cloud to provide end-to-end solutions for our clients. Through the development of an SAP + X reference architecture, we offer a solid foundation for data and analytics needs, tailored to the specific requirements of each organization.

 

The cornerstone of our approach lies in our ability to deliver tangible results quickly and effectively. By employing a "show early and fast" methodology, we engage clients from the outset, facilitating rapid requirement engineering and adjustment. This proactive approach ensures high user satisfaction and overall project success.

 

Our close partnership with SAP enables us to innovate based on evolving technologies while delivering seamless transitions from technology procurement to data-driven decision-making. Regular customer engagements, coupled with our extensive SAP business expertise, allow us to anticipate and address the evolving needs of our clients. Learn more about recent SAP Datasphere innovations in this blog posting from JG Chirapurath, CMO for Business Technology Platform, SAP   

 

At Capgemini, we recognize the strategic importance of a robust business data fabric and tight integration between SAP, its partners, and customers. SAP Datasphere, in conjunction with SAP Analytics Cloud and partner solutions, forms the bedrock for informed decision-making. We advocate for a centralized approach to SAP data, supplemented by hyperscale platforms and external data sources to provide a comprehensive view of business operations.

Capgemini’s commitment to excellence, coupled with our deep-rooted expertise in SAP and data analytics, positions us as a trusted partner in driving business transformation. Together, we navigate the complexities of the digital age, empowering organizations to thrive in an ever-evolving landscape.

 

Join us on the journey to unlock the full potential of your business with Capgemini and SAP.

entry.title: Re: Exposed Data access in Power BI and Other Clients, To...
entry.content:
Hello @w_lizama,

Yes, you can use the views that are Exposed for Consumption with Open SQL Schema via ODBC. Even though you cannot use Analytical Models you can use Fact View that you should have like a based-view for your Analytical Model.

Regards,
Tuncay

Do you know what will happen to the active Performance / Development plans that have old goals tied to them?

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: It has never been easier! Connecting cloud apps to Internet and on-premises systems using SAP BT
entry.content:
" "Basis" adjusted it and CR Destination appeared. "

I could not understand that.

In the case of the errors, the "reason" field is there to shortly describe the error while the "message" field is there to provide you with more insight on what's the next step towards solving the problem.

1. reason: ConfigurationError-ReferencedDestinationServiceInstance-NotProvided

In order Transparent Proxy to work properly, it needs access to service binding / credentials of an instance of Destination service where the actual destinations are stored and managed by the respective administrators. You may check the Destination Service Integration on how to do that.

2. ConfigurationError-ReferencedDestinationServiceInstance-DoesNotExist

In this case it looks like relevant configuration has been applied, but it leads to non existing resource, e.g. K8s Secret resource which usually holds the service instance binding / credentials.

3. reason: ConfigurationError-ConnectivityProxyServiceNameNotProvided

In order Transparent Proxy to work properly, it needs to know what's the proxy endpoint of Connectivity Proxy.You may check the Connectivity Proxy Integration on how to do that.

P.S. Further improvements on the integration between these two modules in Kyma is planned. Stay tuned.

Hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: SAP Secure Login Service for SAP GUI Now Available
entry.content:
Hello @Christian_Cohrs ,

currently we enforce in our systems the X.509 based authentication with smart cards (hardware based certificates). That means, if the smart card is not in the card reader, the logon fails.

With the new SAP Logon Service, the user authenticates with his smart card at the IdP, then gets a soft certificate issued by SAP which is valid for a defined period. The soft certificate is then independent from whether the smart card is still inserted or not. Here we see a change which to our knowledge can only be solved by radically reducing the validity of the soft certificate issued by SAP. 

Are there any other options?

Best regards,

Joe

entry.title: SAP Service and Asset Manger 2310 - Delta-Sync Issue because of i18n
entry.content:
Dear community

I am facing a bit of a strange issue. Maybe someone else experienced something common.

Since 1 month we are using SSAM2310. We did some frontend changes which are working fine. But as soon as i try to include the i18n_de file via CIM, the delta-sync is not working anymore. The delta-sync interrupts within 1-2 seconds with following message ("Error: Communication with the server failed for the download operation with status code: 500" / see also appendix). In the common traces and logs i am not able to find any hints which tell me a bit more about this issue. There is also no request which ends up with an error. First 3-4 requests against the ONLINE-Service are working fine, the sync just stops without seeing any errors in the traces.

I already double-checked the i18n file couple of times if there are any spelling-mistakes, cryptic signs or any regexp-problems which can lead to this problem. But everything seems fine to me.

If i remove the entry for i18n in the CIM again and make a deployment, everything is working fine again.

Maybe here is someone who had to deal with a common kind of issue.

Many thanks in advance.

Tobias

entry.title: Re: Compensation issues troubleshooting step by step
entry.content:
Hi @xavierlegarrec ,

We have following issue with the guidelines not showing in the worksheet when changing the language of the comp planner:

  • Guideline is based on LE (we use the code of the object) and Employee class (we use the label of the picklist).
  • Default language is US English. When launching the worksheet, both the form and the ER show the correct guideline
  • When changing to another language, the guideline is empty in the worksheet, but is showing correctly in the ER. 

Any idea what the cause could be? I try to change the value in the guideline to the code or option id of the picklist value, but no success. 

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: What's up with OpenSAP (Downtime?)
entry.content: Thanks for the update and shoutout to the team resolving the issue swiftly.

entry.title: ABAP Trial Container FAIL: process disp+work Dispatcher not running
entry.content:
Hello everyone.
Earlier this week our ABAP Trial Container had to be restarted. Since then, we are unable to bring the system back up, as we encounter the same error over and over again when starting the docker container:

 

 

2024-03-08T14:53:23.927511259Z 08.03.2024 14:53:23
2024-03-08T14:53:23.927516739Z StartWait
2024-03-08T14:53:23.927520486Z FAIL: process disp+work Dispatcher not running
2024-03-08T14:53:23.931830145Z Worker Processes: the start command failed, exit code=2
2024-03-08T14:53:23.931896940Z The service Worker Processes has failed to start and initialization is blocked until the problem is resolved
2024-03-08T14:53:23.931904032Z You can try your luck 10 times until initialization is terminated
2024-03-08T14:53:23.931907959Z Hint: docker containers can be joined by: docker exec -it <name> bash
2024-03-08T14:53:23.931912818Z Hint: HDB license must not be expired
2024-03-08T14:53:23.931916876Z Hint: HDB must be running
2024-03-08T14:53:23.931920443Z Hint: AS ABAP license must be valid
2024-03-08T14:53:23.931924210Z Hit Return to continue

 

From what I've read here I assume that the licenses expired, which would make sense given the uptime. However, I am unsure as which parameters are the correct ones when generating new license files for the HDB and the ASABAP.

The HDB and the AS ABAP for example show two different hardware keys when they are started in the docker container. A fresh docker container image on the other hand has the same hardware key on both licenses in the SLICENSE transaction. Which ones of these is the correct one?

Also, while troubleshooting it seemed that the System No. was updated again and again. Here as well, a fresh install has the same System No. on both existing licenses. Where can I get the correct current System Number for my system? And do I then have to make any adaptions to the license files before copying them into my container as suggested here? 

I have attached the logs of my latest try to run the container.

Thank you very much in advance.

entry.title: Re: abapGit SSSLERR_PEER_CERT_UNTRUSTED
entry.content:
Read first SSL Setup 

  • 510007 - Additional considerations about setting up SSL on Application Server ABAP

entry.title: Re: March Developer Challenge - CloudEvents: Week 1

{
  "type": "sap.s4.tinhtd.info.v1.SaleOrder.Created.v1",
  "datacontenttype": "application/json",
  "specversion": "1.0",
  "source": "https://cloudevent.tinhtd.info/saleorders",
  "subject": "Sales Order 10608090 created",
  "id": "aHR0cHM6Ly9jb21tdW5pdHkuc2FwLmNvbS90N",
  "time": "2024-03-08T13:00:00Z",
  "data": {
    "SaleOrder": "10608090",
    "OrderType": "ZASO",
    "OrdetItems": [
      {
        "ProductId":"P001",
        "Price":200,
        "Quantity":1
      }
    ]
  }
}

entry.title: Re: SAP Secure Login Service for SAP GUI Now Available
entry.content:
Hi Joe,

actually, you can also continue to use the existing configuration. The product SAP Secure Login Service also includes the scenarios where you only use Secure Login Client, for example based on a smart card.

Best regards,

Christian

entry.title: Re: Get your development through a 3SL landscape with Multi-Off
entry.content: @Karin - Looks like its only for S/4HANA Cloud. Any plans to make this available for BTP ABAP and On-prem?

entry.title: Tips to add your SAP Build Apps application to different BTP environments - Part 1
entry.content:
Hi SAP Builders!

In this blog post, I will showcase 'how to add your deployed SAP Build Apps application to different environments in SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform).

In Part 1, let's see how to do this when all the required services (SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition) are running as part of the same Subaccount in BTP.

Pre-requisites

You are familiar with creating an application using SAP Build Apps and deploying it to the CF (Cloud Foundry) Space of your Subaccount.

For your quick reference, access the SAP Discovery Center and check out the mission ‘Keep the Core Clean Using SAP Build Apps with SAP S/4HANA’. Here in the area ‘Project Board’ under the section ‘DEVELOP’ you can find a tutorial to ‘Create a No-Code Application using SAP Build Apps’.

You may also refer to the SAP Build Apps help portal content to learn about Building a SAP Build Apps application and deploying it.

Verify that the newly deployed app is available in your Subaccount

Before we add your application to the different BTP environments, let us verify that the right version of the app is available in your BTP Subaccount.

For this you need to look up for your app in the HTML5 Applications repository section of your BTP Subaccount.

Spoiler
The direct url of the deployed application can be always found here!
Take a note that the Application Name is constructed as a concatenation of 'buildapps' with your SAP Build Apps project id: '86796'

 

Hint: It is also possible to launch and test the application from here.

 

Integrate your SAP Build Apps application to SAP Build Work Zone, Standard edition

Preparations before integration!

  • Select the SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition from the list of subscription.

NOTE: Only users assigned to the Role Collection: Launchpad_Admin have access permission to SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition to create artifacts at design time.

Refer to Initial Setup | SAP Help Portal -> Procedure-> 3. Assign users to the Launchpad_Admin role collection for some hints in case of any issues while accessing the SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition in the first place.

       
  • From the Channel Manager, refresh the HTML5 Apps to update it with the newly deployed app content.

  • Next, we need to access the Content Manager and go to the Content Explorer and select the HTML5 Apps from there. 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Here you should be able to find your newly deployed SAP Build Apps application!

  • To add your application to the content of this Subaccount, select it and click on Add.
  • Finally, add this application to all the required roles of your end user via Content Manager -> Roles and save it as illustrated below.

 
Now, we are good to get started with adding the application to the different flavors in the SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition.

 Spaces and Pages New Experience     

  • Create a Site using Site Manager, choose the Display mode 'Spaces and Pages - New Experience and add all the necessary settings for this Site and save it. 
  • Create a Page and add the application to it.   
  • Create a Space and add the Page to it.         
  • Add the Space to the Role.
  • Go to Site settings and add all the required role(s) containing this app and this space to the Site and to the end user via BTP Cockpit.
  • Check the Site for this app under this Space and Page and launch it at runtime.

 Classic Launchpad with Groups

  • Create a Site using Site Manager, choose the Display mode 'Groups' and add all the necessary settings for this Site and save it. 
  • Create a group or select an existing Group and add the application to it.
  • Check for the app under this group in the Site and launch the app at runtime.
 
(OR)
  • Create or select an existing Catalog and add the application to it. 
  • Go to the Site settings and add all the required role(s) containing this app to the Site and to the end user via BTP Cockpit.
  • Edit the Launchpad and select the app from Catalog to add it to a new group or under any existing group.                                                            
  • Click on back button or navigate via SAP logo to Home page and launch the app from runtime.

  New Launchpad with Spaces and Pages

  • Create a Site using Site Manager, choose the Display mode 'Spaces and Pages' and add all the necessary settings for this Site and save it.   
  • Create or select an existing Catalog and add the application to it. 
  • Go to the Site settings and add all the necessary role(s) containing this app to the Site and to the end user via BTP Cockpit.
  • Edit the Launchpad and select the app from Catalog to add it to a new section or under any existing section.                                                          
  • Click on back button or navigate via SAP logo to Home page to launch the app from runtime.

With this, I hope that you would also be able add your deployed SAP Build Apps application to SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition.

Spoiler
Coming up next ...
In the Part 2 blog, you will learn to add your deployed SAP Build Apps application to SAP Build Work Zone, Advanced Edition.

Stay tuned and Happy building!

entry.title: Re: Migration effort from PRC 2.0 or 6.0 to S4/HANA
entry.content:
• it is worth to upgrade to PRC 6.0, and again move to S4/HANA?
    • Technically you cannot use the same box for this direct upgrade; as complete deinstallation of PRC2.0 is required before you can lift your system to S/4HANA. How to do this deinstallation you can find out here (https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-blogs-by-sap/how-to-uninstall-sap-product-and-reach-compliance-2-0-to-get-ready-for-sap/ba-p/13462932). I would anyway start as early as possible to upgrade to SAP Product and REACH Compliance (6.0).
  • the effort to migrate from 2.0 or 6.0 to S4/HANA is it the same, or greater in version 2.0, how much different if there's?
    • SPRC2.0 to S/4HANA will not really work or require additional effort.
    • PRC to S/4HANA shall be smooth (in case you have performed the deinstallation already!) 
  • upgrade from 6.0 to S4 is it a simple conversion or complex migration?
    • If you do a big bang, it is quite simply
    • If you plan to lift plant by plant to S/4HANA over several rollouts you will face difficulties

entry.title: Re: A/R Credit Note uses incorrect price to affect Last P...
entry.content:
Hi @Johan_H ,

As I mentioned, by copying the AR Credit from the Invoice, SAP assumes the returning cost is the same as the outbound. If you enable the field "item cost" within form setting, you'll see what is the cost being attributed on both inbound/outbound. Notice in the below screenshot this very scenario. Pay attention to the "Enable Setting Cost" and "Return Cost" greyed out.

However, SAP has a feature that enables the return price to be set manually or according to the price list we want, when the document is not based on another Admin > Sys Initialization > Document Settings > AR Credit. This can be useful for items using Standard cost method, but for moving average and FIFO it might cause some trouble if not handled carefully.

Finally, see below how your your cost can get messed up if you are raising a standalone Credit and set up the return cost to be either based on a price list or manually inputted. The users can simply put the value they think is the correct or the price list configured as showed above might be "doing the job" by itself.

Check whether this applies to your case and let us know.

Hope it makes sense.

Cheers,
Felipe

entry.title: Re: Post with reference
entry.content: See SAP Note 2635096 - Similar journal Entry functionality is not active in Fiori App "Manage Journal Entries" -> https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2635096

entry.title: Remove leading zeros in ABAP select statement
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a below select statement in ABAP

SELECT SINGLE A, B ,C
            FROM lt_tab INTO @DATA(tab1) WHERE B <> C.

But the Column C is having ALPHA conversion with Leading 0's. Hence, Select statement is not working as expected. Could you please help me how we can Remove leading zeros in ABAP select statement at where condition.

 

Regards,

Praveen.

entry.title: Re: VA02 from an ABAP program
entry.content:
@Ryan-Crosby 

Hi Ryan-Crosby,

In my current process, I manually update sales orders through the on-screen transaction code VA02. The steps involved include:

  1. Updating the Order Reason field.
  2. Navigating to the Conditions tab and clicking the Update button.

To automate this process, I am considering two options:

  1. BDC recording: My initial plan was to record a BDC session to replicate the steps performed manually.
  2. BAPI_SALESORDER_CHANGE: However, considering the preference for BAPIs over BDC for standard transactions, I am exploring the possibility of achieving the same outcome using the BAPI_SALESORDER_CHANGE function module.

Before proceeding further, I would appreciate your guidance on whether BAPI_SALESORDER_CHANGE can effectively handle the required updates. Additionally, if you have any insights or recommendations on the most efficient approach for this task, please feel free to share them.

]

entry.title: Re: ADD ON for ERP 6 SAP ERP 6.0
entry.content:
You are on a unsupported version of SAP ERP. Only EHP7 and 8 are currently supported by SAP.

The Netweaver version you have is the 7.31 (SAP_BASIS component).

entry.content: Could I mix plants and storage locations?

entry.title: Adding GL account the FSV
entry.content:
Hello Folks,

I have added new GL accounts using the 'manage GL master data app. The GL was copied from the existing GL as usual and was copied on to the Chart of accounts and company. However when I try to assign this GL to the FSV it does not show up as well. I have tried the classic version as well as the manage global Hierarchies app'. 

This was possible earlier using the 'Copy FSV assignment' anymore. 

The question is how do I assign these unassigned GL accounts to the financial statements? 

 

Regards

Chintan

 

SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

entry.title: CPTA authorization request
entry.content:
Hi team

We are doing some testing after SAP migration to HANA following below steps:

  1. MIGO Goods Issue (coil)
  2. MIGO - Goods Receipt (coil)
  3. QA02 - Enter Lab Test Results
  4. QA32 - Assign Coil Usage Decision
  5. LT10 - Warehouse Movement (coil)
  6. CORZ - Process Confirmation

     When saving we got an error: "You are not authorized to use transaction CPTA". This Tcode is used just for FICO Month end users. Is there a different process we should follow?

entry.title: Re: with PrincipalPropagtion and cloud connector Mobile s...
entry.content: In Mobile Services have you using the Open in Browser to verify the service opens without prompting for authentication?  If the service opens you should remove the ?$auth=uaa and add /$metadata to confirm you can get the metdata for the service.

entry.title: Re: Remove leading zeros in ABAP select statement
entry.content:
Did you try to use Regular Expression in the where clause?

  • LIKE_REGEXPR

entry.title: Re: Way of functioning of SAP Jco Library
entry.content:
To your questions:
1. The sender of the tRFC request should realize that the IDoc send operation failed, i.e. the runtime exception is returned to the sender. If the sender was an ABAP system, see transaction SM58 for the asynchronous RFC error log. You should find a corresponding entry there.

2. No, this should not be necessary.

3. What you are trying to do is absolutely what JCo and the IDoc Library are designed for. If the scenario does not work, you have to investigate at the sender side first, why the failed IDoc(s) are not re-transmitted with the same TID after a failure. Of course, it is also important, how you are reacting in your TIDHandler on the received events. JCoServerTIDHandler.checkTID(...) has to return true for this TID, which failed in a previous call. Otherwise the IDoc(s) won't be sent again. If checkTID isn't called with this TID once again, this is a configuration issue (e.g. time interval for retry attempts) or a bug at the sender system side.

entry.title: Re: CSS file is not loaded when deployed to FLP on-premise system

entry.title: Re: Busca de pedido de venda pela data de cancelamento /...
entry.content:
Olá, @vinicius_dezan 

Me parece que o campo CANCELED só se assume o valor "Y" após o documento ter sido atualizado uma última vez após o pedido já ter sido cancelado. Isso significa que ao cancelar o pedido, CANCELED permanecerá como "N" até que algo ou alguem vá até o pedido e o atualize (pode ser qualquer atualização, como nos comentários por exemplo. Faça um teste).

Acredito que terá de desenvolver algo. Dê uma olhada aqui nesse post. Talvez te ajude na missão.

Query with the date that a sales order was cancell... - SAP Community

Boa sorte!
Felipe

entry.title: Re: Busca de pedido de venda pela data de cancelamento /...
entry.content:
Olá Felipe, tudo bem?

De antemão. obrigado pelo retorno.

No entanto, o CancelDate, infelizmente não refere-se a data de fato de cancelamento do documento, esse campo, serve como uma espécie de "validade" do pedido, onde ele atribui uma data de "Vencimento" do mesmo, no caso em nosso cenário, sempre com 30 dias a contas da abertura do pedido de venda.

Hoje o SAP como Core, trata somente o cancelamento através da tabela de logs (ADOC), com sendo a data do cancelamento, o registro de updatedate do log, na alteração do CANCELED para Y, todavia, não consegui fazer com o parâmetro fosse vinculado a ela, para que a busca fosse feita diretamente por esta.

Atenciosamente,
Vinicius Dezan.

entry.title: Consume international trade data of products from an external system
entry.content:
In product master data in S/4HANA Cloud, there is a classification section, in which master data pertaining to international trade is maintained. This includes commodity codes and customs tariff numbers for example. In the standard public APIs available for reading product master data in S/4HANA Cloud from an external system, these international trade details are not accessible to be read as part of the API payload. To achieve the desired result, a possible consulting solution is to create a custom CDS View, which can be published as a custom external API and later consumed from an external system much like any other public API.

In this blog, I’ll be using the customs tariff details as an example to demonstrate the steps involved to achieve the desired results.

  • In the app “Custom CDS View”, create a new custom CDS view of the category external API.

  • As a data dource, choose ‘C_ProdCstmsTrifNmbrForKeyDate’
  • Under the “Parameters” tab, remove the default parameter P_KeyDate
  • Under the “Elements” tab, you can add additional fields such as CustomsTariffNumber, Country or ValidityEndDate that you might want to consume through the external API.
  • Publish the Custom CDS View.

The next step involves creating a custom communication scenario and arrangement in order to expose the data from the Custom CDS View as an external API. As a prerequisite, a communication system and user will also be required (a guide on how to create these can be found here).

  • In the app “Custom Communication Scenarios”, create a new scenario. Give it a name, and in the inbound services section, add the ID of the Custom CDS View that was previously created.
 

  • Save and publish the scenario.
  • Next, create a communication arrangement. In the app “Communication Arrangements”, create a new one and select the custom scenario ID from the previous step.
 

 

  • Choose the communication system and inbound user you would have created as a prerequisite and save the communication arrangement.
 

  • You can now use the service URL as the API endpoint while consuming data from this Custom CDS View from an external system. The credentials of the communication user will be used to authorize the external system to read this data.
  • At the end of the Service URL, you will need to append the name of the custom CDS View, so your final endpoint URl would look something like this:
    https://myxxxxxx-api.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata/sap/YY1_CUSTOMSTARIFFAPI_CDS/YY1_CustomsTariffAPI
  • As a test, you can use Postman to simulate the result of reading the data from this Custom CDS View. In the Authorizations tab, you will need to specify your inbound communication user and password that were created as a prerequisite.

Hope this blog showed you how you can consume international trade details of products in S/4HANA Cloud from an external system.

entry.title: Receive and present a clear picture of your process analysis to your business executives
entry.content:
Dear all,

We went into General Availability of SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition 3 weeks ago.

We are constantly improving our tool to optimize it and to bring the best version to you. As a result, we are happy to announce our updated PDF Executive Summary for SAP Process Insights, Discovery Edition, which is available for customer on demand.


For those of you, who don’t know the idea from the PDF summary from the now retired Process Discovery for SAP S/4HANA transformation or SAP Innovation and Optimization Pathfinder on Spotlight: it can be used as a support of discussion with stakeholders. It is especially handy when presenting to decision makers, who may not directly use the online solution.

Compared to extended PDF report, you know from Process Discovery and Pathfinder on Spotlight, this executive summary is a crisp document with less than 30 pages, focusing on the mapping of process performance indicators to the relevant SAP S/4HANA capabilities and the right next steps. Hence it is a short summary for every SAP stakeholder and customer, to outline the most valuable SAP S/4HANA recommendations to support the transformation story.


Passed the introduction page, you will find a “TOP Opportunities” page, showing you the 3 highest potential for process optimization, together with their related value driver, the estimated benefits (in monetary value) and the recommendations to achieve these benefits.

 


For each of these value drivers, we selected the indicator of performance with the highest potential for improvement. We mention what your current performance is (for automation rate, for example), and based on SAP experience and best practices, we define a goal -that is realistic and achievable-. How to achieve this goal? By starting a process improvement journey. And how to start this journey? By first implementing the carefully selected correction recommendations (linked on the top right of the page) and innovation recommendations (if you are on SAP ERP, SAP S/4HANA capabilities will be shown, but if you are already live on SAP S/4HANA, we will provide a selection of innovation recommendations, such automation bots or AI).

And as you can see, we show in big blue the potential monetary value that could be realized by achieving the defined target.

This information is directly coming from the Value Analysis section of the SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition.
You don't agree with our maths? Click on "Learn more >>" next to the monetary benefits and it will open the page dedicated to the indicator of performance where you will find our formula, insights coming from your system and SAP assumptions to complement the calculation. You can even amend SAP assumptions to reflect your specific case. Read this blog to know more about the Value Analysis.


The rest of the executive summary is much more familiar to the people, who already explored a Process Discovery analysis. I included a quick summary below as a refresher:


Indicators of performance are in the top area with the green background. These indicators are organized by value drivers (such as "Reduce Finance Costs") under specific Lines of business (here "Finance"). You will find the name of the indicator on which you can click to access the details online. in the coloured cell, you will find the value measured for the indicator. These cells come with different colours, we encourage to focus on the red and white cells in priority. Green means you are one of the leaders of the industry for this indicator. Yellow: you are in the average. And red highlight a potential of improvement. White means we do not have benchmarks yet for this indicators. We currently offer only benchmarks for automation rates. SAP Signavio is working on developing a solution to offer industry benchmarks, we will announce something later.

Recommendations for SAP S/4HANA capabilities are below in the blue area. You can click on them to get more information online. Please bear in mind that every click on this page will lead to online information, and not to a "Details" page within the PDF document. Hence we now speak about :Executive Summary" and not "Report: anymore.


You will find the list of every SAP S/4HANA recommendations for the related line of business after the "Line of Business Summary" page.

 

We completely redesigned the “Next Steps” section, leveraging the latest and greatest, such as the Plug and Gain approach -to guide you in your process transformation:

 

And the SAP Customer Evolution Kit for SAP S/4HANA - To assist you with your move to SAP S/4HANA:


How to receive this PDF Executive Summary?

The Executive Summary is currently available on request at Signavio-Discovery@sap.com. We will let you know when it will be delivered together with the access rights to the online solution (SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition). It is planned for in a couple weeks from now.

You can request SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition for your productive SAP ERP or SAP S/4HANA system on https://url.sap/discovery-edition.

 

I hope this brings some clarity and am very much looking forward to provide you with your PDF Executive Summary very soon!

Kind regards

Rene Cedat
Discovery team
SAP Signavio

entry.title: Reverting CI-DS agent upgrade
entry.content:
Hello,

Can anyone tell me if we can revert the CI-DS agent upgrade if unsuccessful ?

The current agent version is 2306 and it was installed on Windows based server. We are planning to upgrade to 2309 as suggested by SAP to address few issues that we have with CI-DS tasks.

In this regard, we wanted to know if the issues are not solved even after agent upgrade, can we revert to older version to make sure there is no impact to the business ?

Thanks.

entry.title: Cargo Tracing and Intrastat
entry.content:
In my previous blog (How to Run Intrastat in Sap Business One) I explained the Intrastat feature in SAP Business One. This time I want to continue with additional function that has been released recently – cargo tracing according to Country of Origin (CoO).

The requirement origin was raised based on the changes in the Intrastat declaration introduced in many EU countries to report the CoO in import and export reports.

So, to use this function, you need to enable that in the Intrastat configuration:

As you see in this picture the check box the grayed out: enabling this function it is not possible to switch it off. The tracking will be handled to the Intrastat Inventory items only. This function is available for all databases: new and installed one. However, the consistent stock transactions and inventory status is required. Otherwise, you will not be able to enable this function.

In case you have already enabled this function and want to enable the Intrastat to one of the Inventory items the consistency requirement is still actual. Why? During enabling this function B1 runs through all the transactions, except Pick List, Stock Revaluation and Landed Cost. In case there is inconsistency, like negative stock, B1 not able to assign the Country.

Once you enable this function your user can continue as usual with small exception. Let’s look how the system behavior changes.

User receives the stock with Intrastat Relevant item. This transaction depending on the business case can be recorder with Goods Receipt, Goods Receipt PO, Return, A/P Invoice, A/R Credit Memo or with Delivery/ A/R Invoice with negative quantity. In my example this will be the Goods Receipt PO. User receives such document like a standalone or copied from the previous marketing document:

The CoO is taken from Item Master Data as usual. And as usual you can update the CoO directly in the marketing document line:

 Once user presses the Add button, he will receive a new system message:

 In case you do not want any change in B1 behavior you can tick on the Remember My Answer and Do Not Show This Message Again and choose the No answer. Then all the Cargo Tracking relevant tables will be fill out by SAP B1 automatically with the data from the Marketing Document line (Quantity and Country/Region of Origin). These tables are OTNL, OTCN, TCN1, TCN2.

What happens if you want to update the country-of-origin distribution, meaning that you have additional Certificates of Origin for the items? Then press the Yes button. Then Country of Origin – Assignment window will pop-up.

 In this window you can see all intrastat relevant items. Choosing the relevant line, you can update Country of Origin and quantity. In case some of the items are OK, you can leave them with no update. The Country of Origin will be copied to the target document automatically.

Described behavior is common for all in-warehouse transactions. Certainly, the issue is a little bit different for the out-from-warehouse transactions. The main difference is that B1 will not take the Country/Region of Origin form the line to fill out the Cargo Tracking relevant tables. However, you can let SAP B1 to define it automatically in the same way as for the in-warehouse transactions. Then SAP B1 will release the Country of Origin using the FIFO method.

In case you enable cargo tracking in the Intrastat Configuration the Country of Origin will be taken form the Country of Origin – Assignment window.

This feature was released in the SAP Business One 10.0 FP 2208 with the Note  3223246 (Cargo Tracking and Tracing). I hope you find this blog helpful and I look forward to hearing your feedback on your experience working with Intrastat, be sure to leave your comment below.

entry.title: Re: Busca de pedido de venda pela data de cancelamento /...
entry.content: Enquanto você postava sua resposta, eu estava editando meu comentário rs. Realmente, CancelDate tem a funcionalidade que citou e percebi isso só após ter postado. Dê uma olhada lá. De todo modo, espero que te ajude!

entry.title: Re: Busca de pedido de venda pela data de cancelamento /...
entry.content: Opa eu estava chegando a essa questão que mencionou e foi ai que me complicou mais ainda o meu cenário atual kkkk, todavia, vou dar uma avaliada no link passado para conferir, novamente muito obrigado pela ajuda..

entry.title: Production order confirmation using barcode
entry.content:
I would like to use barcodes to confirm production order operations. Can you please give me more details about how to execute this?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Production order confirmation using barcode
entry.content:
Hi,

To confirm a production order using the barcode, you can use the 'Confirm Production Operation' app. In this app there is the Barcode Scanner button, by clicking this button you can use the camera of your mobile device for scanning the order, operation, and suboperation that you want to confirm.

Note that any USB-connected scanning device still works directly without having to select this button.

For more information on how to generate correct barcodes, see How to Generate Barcodes.

For more information on how to enable Barcodes in S/4HANA Cloud, see 3331484 - How to enable Barcodes in S/4HANA Cloud.

Best regards.

entry.title: Re: Define Custom Routines for Data Transfer to Billing D...
entry.content:
Solved. 

Steps for implementing the solution:

0 Implement BADI SD_BIL_DATA_TRANSFER, set up Filter DATA_TRANSFER = 3009999

1 Manage Your Solution ->  go to Configuration Activities tab -> Define Custom Routines for Data Transfer to Billing Documents, open app

2 Create Registration of a Routine Number with 3009999

3 Go to Assign Enhancement IDs to a Routine Number tab and Assign Enhancement IDs to a Routine Number, Save and add to Transport Request

4 As i am creating Billing Document based on Outbound Delivery, then go to Manage Your Solution -> Define Copying Control for Delivery Document to Billing Document

5 My Billing Document has type F2 Invoice, and Items has type CBXN. So i need set up configuration for these types. So, choose Invoice Type

6 Choose appropriate Item type, jump into it

7 Set up Custom Data Transfer = 3009999

Save, add to Transport Request. 

These actions says to system to what Billing Document Type and which Item Type you want to apply implemented BADI.

BR

Vitali

entry.title: EWM - SOS reference in ODO
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am creating Sales Order and a outbound Delivery with respect to it. When ERP Delivery is saved, it is distributed to EWM. It has Stock reference as Sales Order Number and Item.

Can anyone please tell me how to create EWM Delivery without any Stock Reference. 

Thanks !!

entry.title: SAP Launches SAP Analytics Cloud on Apple Vision Pro on Day One
entry.content:
SAP Analytics Cloud has been available in the Apple Vision Pro App Store since February 2 allowing SAC users to experience their mobile ready stories on the Apple Vision Pro. 

The app offers an optimized viewing experience for Apple Vision Pro facilitating the interaction and exploration of business data in a novel user-friendly way. The pairing of SAP Analytics Cloud with Apple Vision Pro represents a striking example of how technology can be harnessed to revolutionize business solutions. 

Want in-depth interaction and personalized touch with your stories? The SAP Analytics Cloud app delivers it right to you. The in-built functionalities allow you to dive into your data with tools like filter selection and drill-down. These features empower users by giving them both the capability and the controls to strategically understand and extract maximum relevance from their data stories. 

But that's not all. Create your own boards based on the charts most relevant to you by simply using drag-and-drop that makes the creation of new boards a seamless experience. 

To fully utilize the benefits of SAP Analytics Cloud mobile app on Apple Vision Pro, you need to be a user of the SAP Analytics Cloud solution, with mobile services enabled by your IT department. The application stands as a remarkable example of how technology and innovation can blend to create a new dimension of convenience, utility, and efficiency in handling business data. 

SAP Analytics Cloud Spatial Boardroom Demo 

Furthermore, SAP Analytics Cloud on Apple Vision Pro presents an impressive addition - the Spatial Boardroom demo. This innovative feature gives us a glimpse into the future of business data visualization and interaction. Step into a reimagined boardroom where 'people data' comes alive through 3D elements and spatial filters. Conveniently, there's no need to set up an account to use this demo. Even though this demo isn't a standalone product, the potential it uncovers for a futuristic custom development project on existing SAP Analytics Cloud technology is truly impressive. 

Adding to its appeal is the Apple Shareplay integration, resulting in a data analysis experience that fosters collaboration with multiple users simultaneously. The Spatial Boardroom demo isn't just a peek into what's possible, it's a promising stride towards the future of business data handling! 

Don't miss out on the future of business data interaction – experience SAP Analytics Cloud on Apple Vision Pro today!

entry.title: Re: New features in ILM Advisor application as part of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402.
entry.content:
Hi Suhas101, Hi Vinod_C,

Here there are two points to be considered.

1. These graphs are built using CDS, there is a well-known issue(feature). The default size limit will be 100 for all models.

https://ui5.sap.com/#/api/sap.ui.model.Model/methods/setSizeLimit

2. The CDS is sorted based on ILM objects, Hence MM_EKKO takes priority over SD_VBRK / SD_VBAK.

 

In future release this limitation of 100 will be addressed.

 

Best Regards,

Guru

entry.title: Re: How to Connect SAP Build Apps to Demo S/4HANA Data
entry.content: Very useful. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: How to make Mandatory a Field
entry.content: There are many ways simple way to achieve this by using Tcode SHD0 and transaction variant.Many articles around this issue check this article - A Step by Step Guide for Creation - Code Gallery - SCN Wiki

entry.title: Efficiency and Insights with Calculation Runs in SAP SuccessFactors Incentive Management
entry.content:
In the dynamic realm of compensation management, efficiency and insight are paramount. Compensation administrators are constantly seeking tools that not only streamline their processes but also empower them with actionable data to make informed decisions. Enter the Calculation Run workspace in SAP SuccessFactors Incentive Management solution – a game-changer poised to revolutionize how compensation administrators operate.

 

Streamlined Navigation:

A hallmark of the Calculation Run workspace is its streamlined navigation. Recognizing the value of time in the fast-paced world of compensation management, SAP SuccessFactors has prioritized user experience to ensure effortless access to critical information.

At the heart of this enhancement lies a dashboard designed with user convenience in mind. Leveraging a card-based design, the dashboard offers a comprehensive overview of ongoing activities, enabling administrators to swiftly grasp the status quo.

The "Running Now" card provides real-time visibility into currently active jobs, allowing administrators to monitor progress seamlessly. Meanwhile, the "Recent/Today" and "Scheduled" cards ensure users stay abreast of past activities and forthcoming tasks, fostering a sense of continuity and foresight.

Users can now easily navigate through errors using the hierarchical display and quickly identify issues to troubleshoot with effective error management.

Additionally, the dashboard furnishes invaluable insights by displaying progress indicators and estimated completion times based on historical job runs. This predictive element equips administrators with the foresight needed to manage workflows effectively.

 

Automated Job Creation:

Another notable feature of the Calculation Run workspace is its streamlined approach to job creation. Recognizing the significance of efficiency in task execution, SAP has implemented measures to expedite this process.

Automated recurring schedules help free up time and reducing manual intervention. Administrators can set their jobs on autopilot with recurring schedules with automated job scheduling and Templates simplify for streaming the process of creating repeated jobs.

 

Administrators can now create jobs with unparalleled ease, thanks to intuitive workflows and simplified interfaces. By reducing the complexity associated with job creation, SAP SuccessFactors Incentive Management empowers administrators to allocate their time and resources more efficiently, thereby enhancing overall productivity.

 

Historical Insights:

In addition to optimizing workflow efficiency, the Calculation Run workspace offers invaluable historical insights. By leveraging past data and performance metrics, administrators gain a deeper understanding of trends and patterns, enabling them to make data-driven decisions.

These historical insights serve as a compass, guiding administrators in navigating the complexities of compensation management. Whether analyzing past job runs or evaluating performance trends, this feature empowers administrators with the knowledge needed to drive strategic decision-making.

 

Benefits:

  • The benefits of the Calculation Run workspace extend beyond operational enhancements to drive tangible business outcomes:
  • Enhanced User Satisfaction and Customer Retention: By providing administrators with a user-friendly and efficient workspace, SAP SuccessFactors enhances user satisfaction, thereby improving customer retention rates. Satisfied users are more likely to continue utilizing the platform, fostering long-term relationships with customers.
  • Positive Brand Perception: A well-designed and user-friendly workspace reflects positively on the brand perception of SAP SuccessFactors. By prioritizing user experience, the company demonstrates its commitment to customer-centricity, enhancing its reputation in the market.
  • Net New Sales: The improved user experience offered by the Calculation Run workspace attracts new customers, driving net new sales for SAP SuccessFactors. As organizations seek solutions that not only meet their functional requirements but also offer a superior user experience, SAP SuccessFactors stands out as a preferred choice in the market.

 

In conclusion, the Calculation Run workspace in SAP SuccessFactors Incentive Management solution represents a significant leap forward in compensation management technology. By combining streamlined navigation, efficient job creation, historical insights, and tangible business benefits, this innovative tool equips administrators with the resources needed to thrive in an ever-evolving landscape.

entry.title: Prevent user from deploying transports into production system while still allowing deploy into test
entry.content:
Dear Team,

   In SAP Cloud ALM Feature, we want to restrict user from deploying Transport request to Production environment, but he should be able to deploy transport to Quality system. Can you please help us with roles that need to be assigned to the user. Please find below screenshot of current role assigned to the user. Please find attached document for more details.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Prevent user from deploying transports into productio...
entry.content:
Hi,

It is possible to create a custom role collection in SAP BTP subaccount based on the role templates included in role collection Deployment Manager and in the custom role, only include role template

  • imp_cdm_DeploymentManagerTest for deployment to test systems

See KBA 3442954 - How to configure Role Assignment to control user's ability to import Feature transports into Test and/or Production systems - Cloud ALM.

Best regards,
Paula

entry.title: RE - How to send a SOAP message ( Consumer Proxy ) to multiple SAP systems from a SAP ERP system
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts, I have a requirement to send BP from SAP ERP(core) system to EWP(S/4 HANA) and TMS(S/4 HANA).  Both EWM and TMS have object BUSINESSPARTNERSUITEBULKREPLIC and we created service binding in both the systems. Now in ERP Core there is consumer proxy: CO_MDG_BP_RPLCTRQ. I have created logical port for each EWM and TMS.

When I am making logical port for EWM and default, data is following to EWM only. Similarly if I make TMS logical port as default then only data following to TMS.  But my requirement is to send data to both EWM and TMS simultaneously. 

Note: We are not using MDG system.

Please help me if there any technique to achieve this. Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Error while consuming Rest API 'the startDate should...
entry.content: @amo_ry in the entire URL? You would only change it in the parameters of the URL.

entry.title: Re: VA02 from an ABAP program
entry.content: @SantoshB yes, you can most certainly update the order reason code and conditions in the BAPI (assuming it's permitted in the pricing procedure configuration).

entry.title: Ocultar pestañas de la barra superior
entry.content:
Hola buenos días me gustaría saber si puedo ocultar algunas de estás pestañas, por ejemplo la de "Módulos" o que los módulos de esa pestaña se puedan parametrizar como como la ventana que tenemos al lado izquierdo. ¡Saludos!

Hello, good morning, I would like to know if I can hide some of these tabs, for example the "Modules" one or that the modules of that tab can be parameterized like the window we have on the left side. Greetings!

entry.title: Re: NF-e Nota Técnica 2023.004 v1.10: novos campos adicionados à nota fiscal
entry.content:
Olá @jgaia, 

Todos pré-requisitos devem ser implementados para que a solução funcione corretamente.

Abraço,

Luize

entry.title: Hello everyone from an SAP employee
entry.content:
Hi folks,

I'm currently hosting a session introducing new people to SAP and thought it would be great to have them all look at the career corner!

I studied international business and have been working at SAP since 2013. I'd like to ask this group for tips to newcomers who want to consider SAP as a career.

What are some ways to get past the barriers to entry for the ecosystem? I feel like everyone is looking for previous experience - how do you get your foot in the door?

Shout out to @jmmargo !

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the beginner corner!
entry.content:
Hi, my name is Rhalene Duarte, I'm from Brazil and work as an Abap Consultant since 2008...a long time ago 🙂
I really enjoy work as a programmer an now I'm learning a little bit SAP PI because I started working with integrations. 
I'm now living in Portugal and have 2 beautiful daughters'

entry.title: New to SAP @Success Factors
entry.content:
How to find trainee jobs when my local market makes it hard to enter due to the age barrier? Maybe tips on how to get more experience with online trainee or internship jobs. This would be highly appreciated. Thank you in advance.

Best regards,

Elisabeta

entry.title: SAP Internship Searching
entry.content:
Hi everyone!My name is Candy Chan,a master student of computer science and i will graduated in the Sep-2024.This is my first time to know the information of SAP and consider the future career based on the information that shared with SAP team.

    I will study the free training after the meeting but hope to know that whether have any online intern that i can start from May in Malaysia.

    Would you mind to share any information or advices based on above details?

   Have a good day!

entry.title: ABAP developer everyday tasks
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm Eloise, and I'm thrilled to be joining this group!

I studied business administration and worked as a payroll administrator, but last year I decided to do quite a career change to become a developer. Currently, I'm upskilling in ABAP Programming. 


Could someone please shed some light on the everyday tasks of an ABAP developer? I'm eager to gain insights into the day-to-day responsibilities and challenges faced in this role. Your input would be greatly appreciated!


Apparently,  tagging @jmmargo might make him happy 😄

entry.title: Re: Hello everyone from an SAP employee
entry.content: 😉

entry.title: Re: Hello everyone from an SAP employee
entry.content:
Hi, my name is Rhalene Duarte, I'm from Brazil and work as an Abap Consultant since 2008...a long time ago 🙂

I really enjoy work as a programmer an now I'm learning a little bit SAP PI because I started working with integrations. 
Show that you are confident and committed with the job is the a good way to start. 
I would like to became a PI and a certification will help me a lot.
@jmmargo

entry.title: Looking for AI related projects with github links to contribute to
entry.content: Looking for AI-related projects with github @jmmargo

entry.title: Re: Hello everyone from an SAP employee
entry.content: I think we can try to connect our previous experience in other industries with SAP, and explain how the company can benefit from our perspectives.  For students who do not have experience, we can show our effort and determination with some certificates and personal projects, etc..  @jmmargo

entry.title: Re: How To Analyze Not Distributed Price Differences with Value Flow Monitor (CKMVFM)
entry.content:
would there be a reason why the sub. Adj. Price Difference is 0 (zero) but the not distributed amount on CKMVFM is showing a number? I am trying to figure out what is the system calculating in this instance. 

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Hello everyone from an SAP employee
entry.content:
Hi David, 

I'm Lucía, I am an Industrial engineer have an strong background in Logistics area. I started Master's in Logistics Project Management SAP and I hope to find soon an internship to start my consulting career. I think is a good idea receive datails about consulting job and opportunities in Logistic Area.

entry.title: Hello SAP community!
entry.content:
I am a relative newbie to SAP community and eager to learn, collaborate and contribute to the community.

I have exposure to SAP S/4HANA Finance before at previous places of work as well SAP training academy but only in limited capacities. I am very much looking forward to learning so much more to enhance my skills and employment prospects as entry functional consultant. Currently pursuing an MBA in Denmark, and have a background in Business economics and IT.

Feel free to connect as I am looking to build my community here.

Best regards,

Beatrice R.

entry.title: Re: Replicate B2B Customer from Sap Commerce to Erp - Customer ID not generated
entry.content: Hi @crescenzorega, How did you handle this issue? Did you change the implementation, or the business process to create the customers?

entry.title: SAP Data & Analytics certifications
entry.content:
Hello everyone, this is Mayra and I am new here. I have a bachelor in Actuarial Science with experience in SAP environments and 6 years in the data field. I'm looking for some certificacions in SAP Data & Analytics, do you have any recomendation of  which certification I should get first? Also any learning sites to prepare for it?

Thanks a lot
Mayra

entry.title: Re: Welcome to Career Corner
entry.content: I graduated from HEC Paris and ESCP, used to be a data analyst in L'Oreal and Mckinsey, looking for data-related job or consulting jobs in Germany @jmmargo

entry.title: Re: Could not fetch client credentials token for service...
entry.content:
Hi KP_1111237,

Did you find a solution for this?

I'll be waiting.

entry.title: Re: Remove leading zeros in ABAP select statement
entry.content: SELECT is working as expected (it never considers the conversion routines, including ALPHA), but you expect to find a way to simulate the ALPHA conversion routine via an SQL function. As you may know, the available SQL functions highly depend on the ABAP version you are using, so you should indicate your ABAP version.

entry.title: Re: Error executing action: Could not fetch client credentials token for service of type "desti
entry.content: Hi MioYasuatke, Did you find a solution for this? I'll be waiting.

entry.title: Re: Error executing action: Could not fetch client credentials token for service of type "desti
entry.content: Hi MioYasuatke, Did you find a solution for this? I'll be waiting.

entry.title: Re: SAP Data & Analytics certifications
entry.content: @jmmargo @daniel_carvalho
